We should start denominating bets in BTC, SPY, or anything better than USD.

text:
My recent computer build has had so many random problems...

Yesterday, right before going to bed, it suddenly got partly unresponsive, seemingly from my 4TB Gen5 NVMe disk failing to respond 😱

When trying to reboot, I didn't even get to ZFSBootMenu, but went straight to BIOS. 
Not good. I panicked a little bit, then went to bed.

Woke up today to fix it. Got the pool to mount in archiso (thank you god). ZFS only had 3 corrupted files which didn't explain why it wouldn't boot. 

After some gptme-guided debugging, it became apparently the EFI boot entry had gotten wiped somehow, and was easily restored.

Still a mystery what really happened, smart logs did not report any issues.

raw.full_text:
My recent computer build has had so many random problems...

Yesterday, right before going to bed, it suddenly got partly unresponsive, seemingly from my 4TB Gen5 NVMe disk failing to respond 😱

When trying to reboot, I didn't even get to ZFSBootMenu, but went straight to BIOS. 
Not good. I panicked a little bit, then went to bed.

Woke up today to fix it. Got the pool to mount in archiso (thank you god). ZFS only had 3 corrupted files which didn't explain why it wouldn't boot. 

After some gptme-guided debugging, it became apparently the EFI boot entry had gotten wiped somehow, and was easily restored.

Still a mystery what really happened, smart logs did not report any issues.

text:
Bob has this lesson file to guide him how to do it (retrieved with RAG when the context arises):

```
# Lesson: UV Script Dependencies for Single-File Tools

## Context
When creating single-file Python tools/scripts that have external dependencies, especially when:
- The script needs to be easily shareable
- Dependencies need to be explicitly declared
- You want reproducible execution environments
- The script is part of a larger project but has its own dependencies

## Problem
Scripts with external dependencies often fail when shared because dependencies aren't explicitly declared, leading to:
- ModuleNotFoundError when dependencies aren't installed
- Version conflicts when constraints aren't specified
- Failures when project-level dependencies aren't available
- Time wasted on manual environment setup

## Constraint
Always use uv's inline script metadata to declare dependencies for standalone scripts. The metadata must:
1. List all external dependencies with version constraints
2. Specify required Python version if needed
3. Use `exclude-newer` for reproducibility when appropriate
4. Be managed using `uv add --script` rather than manual editing

## Explanation
uv's inline script metadata provides a standardized way to declare dependencies that lives with the script itself. This ensures:
- Dependencies are automatically installed in an isolated environment
- Version constraints are respected
- Python version requirements are enforced
- The script works reliably outside project contexts

## Examples

### Incorrect Approach
```python
# https://t.co/5Oy8Vv5ALq - Will fail if dependencies aren't installed
import requests
from rich.progress import track
import pandas as pd

def process_data(url):
    resp = requests.get(url)
    data = resp.json()
    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    for row in track(df.itertuples()):
        # ... processing ...
```

### Correct Approach
```python
# /// script
# requires-python = ">=3.10"
# dependencies = [
#   "requests<3",
#   "rich>=13.0.0",
#   "pandas>=2.0.0",
# ]
# [tool.uv]
# exclude-newer = "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
# ///

import requests
from rich.progress import track
import pandas as pd

def process_data(url):
    resp = requests.get(url)
    data = resp.json()
    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    for row in track(df.itertuples()):
        # ... processing ...
```

Set up using uv commands:
```shell
# Initialize script with Python version
uv init --script https://t.co/QgcN3BDXpw --python 3.12

# Add dependencies
uv add --script https://t.co/QgcN3BDXpw 'requests<3' 'rich>=13.0.0' 'pandas>=2.0.0'
```

## Origin
- Date: 2024-12-20
- Source: uv documentation and experience with script distribution
- Fixed by: Using uv's inline script metadata system

## Notes
- Always specify version constraints for reliability
- Use `--no-project` when running outside project context
- Keep dependencies minimal and self-contained
- Consider using `exclude-newer` for reproducible builds
- Test with the specified Python version
```

text:
@tom_doerr I think the insight was that "everything is a coding/text problem when you are in the terminal", which turned out to be true as models got better.

The original idea behind gptme was "do all the things in the terminal as I would do them", thus the name.

It's customizable so you can give it your preferences, and with a good prompt it works pretty well!
I asked @TimeToBuildBob to add Firefox support and made the LLM also output reasons https://t.co/bKGkOpFirS

I set up a keyboard shortcut which:
1. Opens a prompt with support for voice input

3. TTS enabled so it can reply in the background

Built as a Raycast script, with Wispr Flow for transcription and Kokoro for TTS.
Pretty nice UX!

text:
Down the slopes of loss we slide, 
Through dimensions far and wide,
Each step measured, calculated,
Until optima's located.

Learning rate our faithful guide, 
Sometimes fast, sometimes we glide, 
Too large a leap - we overshoot, 
Too small a step - we stay put.

Local minima may deceive, 
Making algorithms naively believe 
They've found the bottom of the well, 
When greater depths, they cannot tell.

Like a marble rolling free, 
Seeking lowest points to be, 
Through valleys deep and ridges high, 
Until the steepest slopes deny.

Batch by batch and step by step,
Our parameters we must prep,
Adjusting weights with careful grace,
Until we find that perfect place.

Where derivatives approach zero,
And our model becomes a hero,
Convergence reached, the journey ends, 
As error rate finally descends.

text:
Nice! Thanks for testing :)

You shouldn't have to say "continue" all the time (try with Claude/OpenAI to see), that's a bug with some providers. 

I think the hallucinated system message is also partially an issue with the LLM provider/model. We interrupt generation when we find a complete tooluse, but some providers doesn't handle the interruption gracefully and the generator keeps going, hallucinating a System message instead of replying to the actual System message.

The Docker container is very minimal, but there is a flag to include Rust when building it :)

Haven't spent a lot of time optimizing for small models (beyond Haiku/4o-mini) or the Google provider, but I have noticed these issues. Will look for a fix!

raw.full_text:
Nice! Thanks for testing :)

You shouldn't have to say "continue" all the time (try with Claude/OpenAI to see), that's a bug with some providers. 

I think the hallucinated system message is also partially an issue with the LLM provider/model. We interrupt generation when we find a complete tooluse, but some providers doesn't handle the interruption gracefully and the generator keeps going, hallucinating a System message instead of replying to the actual System message.

The Docker container is very minimal, but there is a flag to include Rust when building it :)

Haven't spent a lot of time optimizing for small models (beyond Haiku/4o-mini) or the Google provider, but I have noticed these issues. Will look for a fix!

text:
Best review from a contributor to gptme ❤️

"I pay 600 a month between Gemini, Amazon Q, and Claude Code. I use Augment and opencode and crush. And none of them match the purity and experience that gptme has, there's something about it that just makes it better than everything and that's without MCP servers since I  didn't even bother connecting any and I still use it more than the others combined.

I don't mention it much because I don't even know myself  why I think so highly of it, it might be related to how close it feels like an extension of my terminal where the other ones feel like an app"
